equivalence_argument: No complete mapping: the builder reorders by sequencing-rights priority, but audited code classifies by static/top-level transaction target and does not run the simulated contract call-graph classification required by this invariant.

checked_queries: simulated contract call graph sequencing rights builder
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Payload building does not classify transactions by simulated contract call graph before sequencing-rights reordering.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/payload/src/sequencing_rights.rs classifies candidates with try_classify_static or classify_top_level_with_sr.
crates/ritual-sequencing-rights/src/classifier.rs defines ContractCallInspector and into_classification_with_sr, but search found no production use site.
reorder_payload_candidates falls back on parent-state lookup or reorder_block errors, but not on simulation failures because no simulation is invoked.

notes: Top-level sequencing-rights ordering is present, but the invariant requires call-graph simulation semantics.
rationale: The merge introduces unified block building paths that classify transactions with ContractCallInspector, call reorder_block, and log sequencing-rights phases while preserving fallback ordering on errors. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Payload building must classify transactions by simulated contract call graph, reorder sequencing-rights buckets with reorder_block, and fall back to original/tip order if state lookup, simulation, or reordering fails.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create a block candidate with multiple transactions calling a contract whose sequencingRights ordering prefers selector B before selector A; assert the builder reorders those transactions within that contract bucket while preserving tip order for transactions without sequencing rights or when reordering errors.
summary: Builder sequencing-rights ordering lacks simulated call-graph classification.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create a transaction whose top-level target lacks sequencingRights but an internal call reaches an SR contract; the current builder will not classify it as the SR contract bucket.
title: Builder applies sequencing-rights ordering
